methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $140M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Heron Power, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Drew Baglino, has raised $140M to date. Valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies: one public entity (LNZA) and eight private firms spanning the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $140M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $266M. Peer-median analysis examines six directly comparable Series B climate-tech companies, which span a range from $120M to $5.0B. The median valuation across these peers is $355M, reflecting the sector's wide dispersion—driven partly by outliers like Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B—but anchored by the cluster of $300M–$500M valuations (Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc.).

Synthesizing both methods yields a triangulated range of $226M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $310M. This range reflects reasonable confidence in the $300M–$400M band, though material uncertainty remains given the 40x spread in the peer set and heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital efficiency across climate-tech ventures. The primary valuation driver will be progress on technical de-risking and capital deployment efficiency relative to peer benchmarks.
